summary: Backend-only testing for FinançasPro completed. 15/15 pytest tests passed covering auth (register/login/me/logout), validation (422 for bad email/short password/non-positive value), transactions CRUD + sorted-by-date, goals CRUD + deposit, row-level security across two users, brute force lockout (5 fails => 429 with 'Muitas tentativas'), admin seed login, and health endpoint. Verified bcrypt hash format ($2b$) and all required MongoDB indexes (users.email unique, transactions user_id+date, goals user_id, login_attempts.identifier).

minor.issue: Logout requires valid access_token (depends on get_current_user). If token expired or cookies already cleared, client cannot log out — returns 401. Consider making logout idempotent (best-effort cookie clear).
allow_origins is hardcoded to [FRONTEND_URL, http://localhost:3000]. CORS_ORIGINS env var is set to '*' in .env but not consumed by the code.
Rate-limit key uses request.client.host; behind a proxy/ingress this may collapse to a shared IP and affect other users of the same email. Consider X-Forwarded-For parsing.

action_items: Optional: make POST /api/auth/logout idempotent (don't require valid access_token; just clear cookies).
Optional: honor CORS_ORIGINS env var or remove it to reduce configuration drift.
Optional: consider TTL index on login_attempts (auto-expire after 15 min) instead of only time-window check.
critical_code_review_comments: seed_admin correctly rehashes when env password differs (meets playbook).
All resource endpoints correctly scope queries with user_id (RLS verified across two users).
MongoDB _id correctly excluded with projection {_id: 0} on reads; create_transaction/create_goal also pop _id after insert_one (good).
Cookies use SameSite=None + Secure (correct for cross-origin preview).
Pydantic validations (EmailStr, min_length=6, gt=0) return 422 as expected.
Using FastAPI on_event('startup') is deprecated in newer FastAPI; consider lifespan handlers (non-blocking).
test_credentials: Admin admin@financaspro.com / admin123 verified working. Arbitrary TEST_* users registered during tests.
seed_data_creation: Each test creates its own TEST_<suffix>@x.com users. No seeded fixtures beyond the admin auto-seed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend endpoints all working. Brute-force test adds 6 failed entries for the TEST_bf user's ip:email in login_attempts — they auto-clear after 15 min or upon successful login. If re-running tests and hitting 429 unexpectedly, clear login_attempts collection.
rca of the issue: No issues to RCA — all tests passed.
